title: 3 Cheap Mid-Cap Energy Stocks to Own as Oil Prices Surge to $100
author: Jesse Cohen
description: Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have driven crude oil prices near $100 per barrel, creating favorable conditions for energy stocks. The article highlights three undervalued mid-cap energy companies positioned to benefit from higher oil prices and increased exploration activity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Gulf of Mexico offshore producer with oil-heavy production mix benefits directly from surging oil prices. InvestingPro rates it a Buy with 38.6% upside to fair value, 43.5% EPS growth forecast for 2026, and robust 19.8% free cash flow yield.

Drilling and pressure pumping services provider thrives on increased exploration activity from elevated oil prices. Boasts 61% YTD return, Buy consensus rating, 22.5% fair value upside, and positive post-earnings momentum with Goldman Sachs price target support.

Non-operator with interests in Williston and Permian Basins offers stable, royalty-like revenues from oil-weighted assets. Trading at 9.7 forward P/E (sector discount), 12.7% fair value upside, best Financial Health score in the group, and defensive appeal with steady dividend.

title: Top 10 Most Shorted Stocks: Lucid, MARA, Hims and More
author: Erica Kollmann
description: The article identifies the top 10 most heavily shorted stocks as of December 29, 2025, led by Lucid Group with 54.51% short interest, followed by Choice Hotels and Avis Budget Group. Short sellers believe these companies are overvalued, while bullish traders view high short interest as potential short squeeze opportunities. The list includes stocks with market caps above $2 billion and free floats above 5 million shares.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Highest short interest at 54.51%, indicating strong conviction among professional traders that the company is overvalued and faces serious risks
Second highest short interest at 50.20%, reflecting significant bearish sentiment from institutional investors
Third highest short interest at 48.80%, indicating substantial short seller conviction about declining company value
Fourth highest short interest at 43.14%, suggesting notable bearish positioning by professional traders

36.23% short interest reflects significant bearish sentiment among institutional investors
35.22% short interest indicates substantial short seller positioning and concerns about company valuation
35.11% short interest reflects notable bearish conviction among professional traders
34.27% short interest indicates significant short seller belief in declining company value
33.27% short interest reflects meaningful bearish positioning by institutional investors

title: 3 Mid-Cap Energy Firms Analysts See Moving Up to the Big Leagues
author: Nathan Reiff
description: Analysts highlight three mid-cap energy companies with potential for significant growth amid geopolitical conflicts, renewable energy advances, and strategic operational approaches in the energy sector.

title: Northern Oil And Gas Eyes 2025 Growth Despite Q4 Setbacks: Details
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Northern Oil and Gas expects strong production growth in 2025 despite Q4 2024 setbacks, with plans to increase capital spending and well activity to drive future growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Northern Oil and Gas' optimistic outlook for 2025, with expectations of strong production growth, increased capital spending, and higher well activity to drive future growth, despite some Q4 2024 setbacks.
The article mentions that investors can gain exposure to Northern Oil and Gas through the Invesco S&P SmallCap Energy ETF, but does not provide any specific commentary on the ETF itself.

title: Changes to Social Security We Might See in Trump's Presidency
author: The Motley Fool
description: The article discusses potential changes to Social Security during Donald Trump's second presidential term, including eliminating federal taxes on Social Security benefits, increasing oil and gas drilling to fund the program, and the impact of Trump's proposed policies on Social Security's finances.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that Trump's proposal to increase oil and gas drilling could be used to bolster Social Security's finances, which is viewed as a positive potential change.
